query: What was the conclusion about GraphQL vs REST?

positive.text: After running GraphQL on the product catalog API for six months, Broadleaf Engineering concluded that it introduced more complexity than value for their use case. The flexibility of ad-hoc queries was rarely utilized by mobile clients who made the same three queries, while the N+1 resolution overhead and lack of HTTP caching negated the expected benefits. | Involving: Broadleaf Engineering | An architectural assessment.

negative.text: The product catalog API was reverted from GraphQL back to REST in February 2026. The migration was completed in one sprint because the underlying data layer had remained unchanged. | When: February 2026 | Involving: Broadleaf Engineering | A reversion event.

query: What does the disaster recovery plan specify?

positive.text: Disaster recovery at Summit Cloud requires a Recovery Point Objective of 1 hour and a Recovery Time Objective of 4 hours. query: What has been observed about standup meeting length?

positive.text: Daily standups at Ironwood Labs have been creeping from the intended 15-minute timebox to an average of 28 minutes over the past two months. The primary cause is engineers using standup to discuss implementation details rather than focusing on blockers and status. Teams with a rotating facilitator role tend to stay closer to the 15-minute target. | Involving: Ironwood Labs Engineering | A meeting efficiency observation.
